name: Salvation Army Doughnuts
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon Vanilla powder or vanilla extract.
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the vegetable shortening and sugar until fluffy.
2. Add the eggs and vanilla (if using extract) and beat well.
3. Combine the dry ingredients (including vanilla if using powder) in a separate bowl, then sift together with the baking powder, salt, and flour.
4. Gradually add the dry mixture to the creamed mixture, alternating with milk, until combined.
5. Roll the dough into a 1/2-inch thick round on a floured surface.
6. Cut into rounds using a glass or small prescription pill bottle for center cuts.
7. If desired, cut out centers and roll them together for additional doughnuts, or fry as is for "donut holes."
8. Deep fry in vegetable oil until golden brown, turning once.
9. Drain on a paper towel to absorb excess oil.
10. Dust with confectioner's sugar before serving.
Yield: Depends on the thickness of the rolled dough and whether centers are used to make whole doughnuts or "donut holes."
